scope: Generic object registry and safe metadata-driven integration only. Existing auth, OAuth, JWT, RBAC, FLS, sharing, reports behavior, dashboards behavior, caching, Composite API, frontend routes, API URLs, and response schemas were preserved.

registryDesign.browserExposure: GET /object-registry.js emits window.SaaSRAY_OBJECT_REGISTRY for SPA/report builder use.

hardcodedLogicRemoved: Backend OBJECTS literal is now derived from the object registry.
Primary CRM object list is now derived from registry primary flags.
Internal object list is now derived from registry internal flags.
Startup sf_objects labels now come from registry labels instead of object-name ternaries.
Report metadata object labels now come from registry labels.
Report metadata object eligibility now uses supportsReports.
Report cross-filter relationships now come from registry reportRelationships.
Standard related list definitions now come from registry relatedLists.
Frontend app object metadata is overlaid from the generated registry.
Report builder relationship options now read browser registry relationships.

backend: Existing generic routes still use OBJECTS, but OBJECTS is now registry-generated.
Related list rendering can consume registry relationship definitions for standard child-object lookups.
Reports and dashboard report metadata can discover registered reportable objects through registry flags.
frontend: The CRM SPA loads object-registry.js before app.js.
Existing OBJECT_META remains backward-compatible and is merged with registry metadata.
Reports page loads object-registry.js and derives custom report/cross-filter relationships from it.
newObjectPath: The backend list/detail/search/report metadata paths can consume the new registry entry without route duplication.

remainingHardcodedAreas: Global SOSL RETURNING clauses still use explicit object field lists to preserve current search response behavior.
Salesforce key-prefix object detection remains explicit because key prefixes are Salesforce-specific and should be validated per org before registry-only use.
Special related list logic remains for OpportunityContactRole and org-specific Opportunity-to-Case lookup detection.
Some report builder preferred-field display ordering remains hardcoded for user experience consistency.
Object-specific detail layout sections in public/app.js remain as UI layout definitions, not route/API behavior.
